races.name: ΔΕΗ Tour of Hellas — Stage 3
races.description: Stage 3 is the queen stage of the 21st ΔΕΗ Tour of Hellas — the hardest day of the race with 207.3km and a brutal 4,216m of elevation gain, by far the biggest climbing day in the 2026 edition. The stage departs from Volos, the coastal city in Thessaly (home of the legendary Argo and gateway to Mount Pelion), and finishes in Lamia after traversing the mountains of Central Greece. This stage will decisively shape the GC. GC leader Mathis Avondts (Azerion / Villa Valkenburg, BEL) held on after Stage 1 but Jan-Willem van Schip (his teammate and the Stage 1 breakaway survivor) was DISQUALIFIED before Stage 2 for undisclosed reasons. Stage 2 (Karpenisi → Larissa) was won by Dušan Rajović (Solution Tech NIPPO Rali, SRB) in a bunch sprint — his 11th win of the season — after the early five-man break was caught 3km from the finish. Livestream available via Team Vorarlberg partner k19.at from 13:30 local.

gc_after_stage_2.notes: Avondts leads after S1 sprint win. Van Schip (Azerion) DSQ'd before S2. S2 won by Rajović (SNRI) in bunch sprint.

key_riders: Mathis Avondts (Azerion / Villa Valkenburg) — GC leader
Dušan Rajović (Solution Tech NIPPO Rali) — S2 winner

broadcasts.notes: Greek national broadcaster ERT and official race website. Stream may be globally accessible.
Austrian livestream partner (Team Vorarlberg) streaming from 13:30 CEST. Full stage replays on YouTube (Tiz-Cycling) same day.

races.name: Giro d'Italia — Stage 1 (Grande Partenza)
races.finish_type: Bunch sprint — flat finish on Burgas Black Sea waterfront
races.description: The 109th Giro d'Italia gets underway with its Grande Partenza in Bulgaria for the first time — the 22nd nation to host a Giro stage and 12th foreign country to host the Grande Partenza. The 147km opening stage rolls out from UNESCO World Heritage-listed Nessebar (a 3,000-year-old medieval town on a Black Sea peninsula) along the Bulgarian Black Sea coastline north through Sunny Beach, down into Burgas (pop. 200,000), and through a midpoint 22km circuit near Sozopol ridden twice, before the final sprint finish along Burgas' seafront. The stage is almost entirely flat with only a token 1.1km 'climb' at 2.5% to generate a KOM competition. A high-speed sprint is virtually guaranteed. Jonathan Milan (Lidl-Trek) is the pre-stage favourite to take the first Maglia Rosa. Controversy and ill-health have clouded the build-up: a manure-spreading incident near a team hotel has left multiple riders ill, with Arnaud De Lie (Lotto Intermarché) reported among those affected by gastroenteritis. 23 teams (18 WT + 5 Pro), 184 riders. Stages 1–3 in Bulgaria, then rest day May 11, then Italy from May 12 through to Rome on May 31.
sprint_favourites: Jonathan Milan (Lidl-Trek) — standout favourite, wants his first Maglia Rosa
Tobias Lund Andresen (Decathlon CMA CGM Team)
Arnaud De Lie (Lotto Intermarché) — reportedly ill
gc_favourites: Jonas Vingegaard (Visma | Lease a Bike) — pre-race favourite, seeking the final GT jersey
Giulio Pellizzari (Red Bull-BORA-hansgrohe) — Italian home hopes
races.route_notes: Stage 2 (May 9): Burgas → Veliko Tarnovo, 219km. Stage 3 (May 10): Plovdiv → Sofia, 175km. Rest day May 11. First Italian summit finish: Blockhaus (Stage 7, May 15, 244km).

races.name: La Vuelta Femenina by Carrefour.es — Stage 6
races.finish_type: Mountaintop — Les Praeres de Nava (3.7km @ 13.4% avg, max 27%)
races.description: The first true summit finish of La Vuelta Femenina 2026 — and the stage where the GC will shift from sprinting hands to climbing hands. Stage 6 runs 106.5km from Gijón (capital of Asturias) to Les Praeres de Nava: a brutal wall of 3.7km averaging 13.4% with a maximum ramp of 27%, making it one of the most savage short climbs in professional cycling. Lotte Kopecky (SD Worx-Protime) enters in red after an outstanding opening week that has included SD Worx-Protime stage one-twos on both Stages 4 and 5. Kopecky leads Franziska Koch (FDJ United-SUEZ) by 12 seconds, Cédrine Kerbaol (EF Education-Oatly) by 18s, Mischa Bredewold (SD Worx) by 22s, and Anna van der Breggen (SD Worx) by 26s. Pauline Ferrand-Prévot (Visma-LB) and Kasia Niewiadoma (Canyon-SRAM) are the primary climbing threats. Marianne Vos (Visma-LB) has abandoned the race. Stage 7 (tomorrow, Angliru) is the final stage and will decide the overall winner.
stage_winners_so_far: S1: Noemi Rüegg (EF Education-Oatly) — uphill sprint
S3: Cédrine Kerbaol (EF Education-Oatly) — solo raid
S5: Mischa Bredewold (SD Worx-Protime) — sprint (Kopecky eased up)
key_climbers: Pauline Ferrand-Prévot (Visma | Lease a Bike) — stage pick, explosive climber
Anna van der Breggen (SD Worx-Protime) — 5th at +0:26, designated team climber
Lotte Kopecky (SD Worx) — red jersey, must limit losses on 13% gradients
